derived.causal_summary: Remediated the deep-review findings on the relation-inference backfill. The data-integrity defect (SEC-001): collectSupersessionEdges emits 'contradicts' for predecessor->successor and collectLineageEdges emits 'caused' for the SAME directed pair (lineage is reciprocal), so a committed contradicts:true run inserted caused first then triggered detectContradictions->invalidateEdge, silently setting invalid_at on the just-created valid caused edge (and could invalidate a pre-existing manual edge). Fix: a relation-agnostic conflict guard hasConflictingValidEdge(db, source, target, relation) reuses the now-exported relationsConflict and, inside the same transaction, skips any conflict-prone backfill auto edge whose pair already has a VALID conflicting edge; skipped edges are reported under a new skippedConflicting field and never counted as written. The contradicts direction/labeling is intentional and unchanged (ADR-001). COR-002: the inner backfill schema is now built via getSchema so it rejects unknown keys instead of silently dropping typos. COR-001: written/byRelation are derived from a before/after countValidAutoEdgesByRelation delta of valid created_by='auto' edges, so re-runs report written=0 and invalidated edges are excluded. Maintainability P2s cleared: unused createSpecDocumentChain import dropped, dead bumpRelation no-op loop removed, spec-chain strength literals named, a single insertInferredEdges helper extracted. New relation-backfill-conflict.vitest.ts proves all cases; tsc clean; 179 tests green across 9 suites. Commit + deploy stay user-gated.
